topic: Someone receives a friend request from a childhood bully
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification popped up on my screen, a stark, unwelcome intrusion into my otherwise quiet afternoon. My stomach immediately tightened into a knot. It was a profile picture I hadn't seen in over a decade, a grainy, overly-confident grin that instantly dragged back the memory of playground taunts and whispered cruelties. My fingers hovered over the 'Accept' button, trembling slightly. A wave of heat rushed up my neck, making my skin prickle. I stared at the screen, the silence of my room amplifying the frantic drumming in my chest. *Why now?* The thought echoed hollowly. I quickly minimized the message, shoving my phone under a cushion, trying to physically shrink away from the unwelcome connection.

[story 2]

I nearly dropped my coffee mug. The screen glowed with the familiar, mocking name. Every muscle in my jaw clenched so tightly I thought my teeth might crack. It felt like a physical blow, a sudden drop in temperature followed by a searing flush across my cheeks. I reread the request three times, each repetition a fresh assault on my composure. I could feel my breath catching in my throat, shallow and fast. My hands began to shake visibly as I navigated to the profile, my eyes darting over the carefully curated facade. A profound sense of exposure settled over me, heavy and suffocating. I ended up just staring at the profile picture until the urge to throw the device across the room became overwhelming.

[story 3]

When the notification arrived, I froze mid-sentence, my pen hovering over a blank page. The air in the room seemed to thicken, pressing down on my chest. A strange, unpleasant clenching started low in my abdomen and spread outward, leaving a strange, hollow ache. I slowly turned my chair around, the movement stiff and jerky. Looking at the request felt like stepping back into a spotlight I desperately wanted to escape. My gaze snagged on the familiar face, and a tightness formed behind my eyes. I swallowed hard, the sound loud in the quiet space, and felt an intense need to disappear, to vanish into the wallpaper. I quickly closed the browser tab, the act feeling clumsy and desperate.
